sections.title: Before learning about Mage Training Arena

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Greetings. What wisdom do you seek?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm new to this place. Where am I?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Well young one, you have entered the Magic Training Arena. It was built at the start of the Fifth Age, when runestones were first discovered. It was made because of the many pointless accidents caused by inexperienced

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: mages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who created it?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Good question. It was originally made by the ancestors of the wizards in the Wizards Tower. However, it was destroyed by melee and ranged warriors who took offence at the use of this new 'Magic Art'. Recently,

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: the current denizens of the Wizards Tower have resurrected the arena including various Guardians you will see as you look around. We are here to help and to ensure things run smoothly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. So what can I do here?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: You may train up your skill in the magic arts by travelling through one of the portals at the back of this entrance hall. By training up in one of these areas you will be awarded special Pizazz Points unique to

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: each room. With these points you may claim a variety of items from my fellow guardian up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you record the points I have earned?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: You really are full of questions! You will need a special Pizazz Progress Hat! I can give you one if you so wish to train here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please!

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Ah, I'm afraid I can't give it to you as you don't have room to carry it!

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Here you go. Talk to the hat to find out your current Pizazz Point totals.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Talk to it?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Well of course, it's a magic Pizazz Progress Hat! Mind your manners though, hats have feelings too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... if you insist.

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Oh, and a word of warning: should you decide to leave the rooms by any method other than the exit portals, you will be teleported to the entrance and have any items that you picked up in the room removed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you explain the different portals?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: They lead to four areas to train your magic: The Telekinetic Theatre, The Alchemists' Playground, The Enchanting Chamber, and The Creature Graveyard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the Telekinetic Theatre?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: In there you can earn Telekinetic Pizazz Points for trade upstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will I be doing in there?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: That depends on how much of a time-waster you are! You are required to use the Telekinetic Grab spell in order to move a statue through a maze. Casting the spell will move the statue towards you until it reaches

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: a wall. So by standing on the different sides of the maze you can move the statue North, East, South or West. You will be rewarded Pizazz Points for each maze successfully solved.

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: It will be clearer when you arrive and there's a Guardian through the portal that will be able to tell you more. There's a book you can buy from the Rewards Guardian upstairs that will explain it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the Alchemists' Playground?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: In the playground you can earn Alchemist Pizazz Points for trading with the rewards guardian upstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's in there?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: You'll find eight cupboards containing items you can turn to gold using the low and high alchemy spells. The money you earn will be taken from you upon leaving the playground to pay for the upkeep of this training

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: arena and to help fund magic shops around Gielinor. You will be rewarded with 1 Pizazz Point for every 100 coins deposited and a percentage of the money you create. Keep in mind that you will not be able to take

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: more than 1000 coins back out with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds simple.

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: I am afraid not, for the cost of the items varies, as does their locations in the cupboards. You'll see a list of item costs that will help you decide what you should be converting to gold.

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Fear not, it will be clearer when you arrive and there's a Guardian through the portal that will be able to tell you more. You can also buy a book from the Rewards Guardian upstairs that will explain it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the Enchanting Chamber?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: In here you will be able to earn Enchantment Pizazz Points for trade upstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will I have to do?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: You will find yourself amongst various piles of shapes. You can pick up these shapes and use one of your enchanting spells upon it to morph it into an orb. You'll also see a hole in the centre of the room; put the orbs

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: in here and for every 20 we will credit you with a gift. You will get Pizazz Points for every 10 shapes that you convert and the amount of points depends on the spell you use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the difference between the shapes?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Ah, you will be notified of a different shape that will give you a bonus Pizazz Point when you convert that shape. You should also look out for dragon stones, as these will give you more Pizazz Points when you

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: convert them.

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: It will be clearer when you arrive and there's a Guardian through the portal that will be able to tell you more. You can also buy a book from the Rewards Guardian upstairs that will explain it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the Creature Graveyard?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: In here you will be able to earn Graveyard Pizazz Points for trade upstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what do I have to do in there?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Patience young one. A great many creatures die in this world and much of their remains can cause clutter. We have taken it upon ourselves to teleport many bones from these creatures into this graveyard for a

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: use. It's up to you to practice your bones to bananas spell in order to put these bones into immediate use - nutritious food for monsters! Just convert the bones and put them in the holes in the walls. There is a

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: drawback to this room however, the bones often fall on people so you may want to eat some of those bananas to stay alive. When people die we confiscate Pizazz Points for the effort of teleporting incompetent mages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What else do I get from this besides bruises?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: As well as the Pizazz Points for giving us the bananas, you will be credited with runestones for your efforts, and you may be able to save up for a new kind of spell to use in the graveyard!

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: It will be clearer when you arrive and one of my fellow guardians through the portal will be able to tell you more. You can also buy a book from the Rewards Guardian upstairs that will explain it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, bye!

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Use what you have learned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: None, I don't really care.

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: You won't gain with that attitude.

sections.title: After receiving the progress hat once

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Greetings. What wisdom do you seek?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about this place again?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Very well. You have entered the magic training arena. It was built at the start of the Fifth Age, when runestones were first discovered. It was built to put an end to many pointless accidents involving inexperienced

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: mages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who created it?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Good question. It was originally made by the ancestors of the wizards in the Wizards Tower. However, it was destroyed by melee and ranged warriors who took offence at the use of this new 'Magic Art'. Recently,

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: the current denizens of the Wizards Tower have resurrected the arena including various Guardians you will see as you look around. We are here to help and to ensure things run smoothly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. So what can I do here?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: You may train up your skill in the magic arts by travelling through one of the portals at the back of this building. By training up in one of these areas you will be awarded Pizazz Points unique to each room. With

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: these points you may claim a variety of items from my fellow guardian up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you record the Pizazz Points I have earned?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: With the Pizazz Progress Hat I gave you, of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Oh, and a word of warning: should you decide to log out whilst in any of the rooms in the arena, you will be teleported to the entrance and have any items that you picked up in the room removed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you explain the different portals?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: You want another one don't you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, can I?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Ah, I'm afraid I can't give it to you as you don't have room to carry it!

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Here you go. Talk to the hat to find out your current Pizazz Point totals.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. What's it for?

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Collect Pizazz Points from the various areas and the hat will remember your totals. Talk to the hat at any time to find out what points you have. Go upstairs and talk to the Rewards Guardian to claim items for the

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: points.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, bye!

lines.speaker: Entrance Guardian
lines.text: Use what you have learned.
